basics.name: Luis Monzón
basics.label: Backend Software Engineer | Distributed Systems | Event-Driven Architectures | Scalable Platforms
basics.summary: Backend Software Engineer with 4+ years of experience building and scaling distributed, event-driven systems. Strong focus on high-throughput architectures (Kafka, RabbitMQ) processing 100K+ events/day. Experienced in end-to-end software lifecycle ownership, including design, implementation, deployment, and production support. Passionate about building reliable, scalable systems with strong performance and operational excellence.

work.name: Minsait
highlights: Owned end-to-end development lifecycle of a distributed billing platform processing 100K+ events/day in production
Designed and implemented event-driven architecture using Kafka and RabbitMQ for scalable, decoupled systems
Improved system performance by 15% through payload optimization and parallel consumer processing
Built microservices for audit, billing, and document management with focus on reliability and extensibility
Designed and implemented resilience patterns including Dead Letter Queues (DLQ) and retry mechanisms
Deployed and operated services in OpenShift, ensuring high availability and production stability
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to define requirements and deliver production-ready features

work.name: Optimissa
highlights: Developed backend systems for large financial institutions (BBVA, BME) in high-reliability environments
Implemented business-critical features using Java, Spring Boot, and Python
Participated in full SDLC: design, development, testing, deployment, and production support
Improved system stability by identifying and resolving critical production incidents
Collaborated with senior engineers in debugging and architectural improvements
